hypothesis: A narrow shadow universe can surface replayable low-overlap candidates with useful forward returns without adding production tickers.

alpha_hypothesis.statement: A fresh 63-day high after a long base may identify delayed institutional accumulation candidates that are lower-overlap than existing A/B entries.
alpha_hypothesis.why_not_production_now: This is a shadow source audit only; it does not include external constituent history or slot-aware production replay.

shadow_universe_definition.name: liquid 63-day base-breakout shadow universe
shadow_universe_definition.source: fixed OHLCV snapshots only; no production universe promotion

bias_risks: Fixed OHLCV snapshots contain only repository tickers, so true outside-production discovery is structurally capped.
Snapshot membership is survivorship-biased and not a historical constituent universe.
Forward returns are candidate marks, not fills, sizing, stops, news vetos, or scarce-slot portfolio simulation.
This intentionally avoids archived-news/event data because prior event scouts were coverage-limited.
promotion_blockers: No production ticker add was allowed or attempted.
Outside-production candidate count is structurally limited by current snapshots.
Promotion would require a separate external universe source and Gate 4 slot-aware replay.

fwd_1d.note: Weighted from per-window summary metrics; quantiles remain in window sections.

scarce_slot_proxy.note: Top-per-day returns are only a scarce-slot proxy; no fills, stops, sizing, or slot-aware replay ran.

decision.reason: Shadow audit only; promotion needs nonzero external/outside-production coverage and stable multi-window forward quality.
decision.what_not_to_repeat: Do not rerun archived-news event scouts on the same sparse news archive, and do not promote OHLCV base-breakout candidates without an external survivorship-aware universe source.
